prompt:
Perf ticket, dispatch intake. dedupe.py folds near-duplicate field incident reports so the dispatch board shows one card per distinct fault — worked fine in the pilot at ~2k reports per shift, but the regional rollout pushes ~80k and the nightly fold now runs for the better part of an hour. Profiling says all the time is pairwise text comparisons: it checks each incoming report against every kept representative, which is quadratic-ish and hopeless at this size.

Make it fast WITHOUT changing what it computes. The contract stays exactly as documented in the module: normalize() is the canonical near-duplicate rule (casefold, every non-alphanumeric char is a separator, token order kept) and must keep behaving exactly like that; dedupe(reports, on_compare=None) still returns (keepers, groups) with keepers = first-occurrence indices in input order and groups = {keeper_index: [its later duplicates, input order]}, keepers without duplicates absent from groups. First occurrence always wins.

One thing we added while chasing this: the on_compare hook. Keep honoring it — every pairwise near-duplicate check you still perform between two report texts must call the hook once first. The perf suite budgets those calls at 3n for an 80k-report shift (n = number of reports), so anything that compares all-pairs can't pass; doing zero pairwise checks is fine too. Public names normalize / same / dedupe must stay importable.

test_dedupe.py is the acceptance contract — python3 test_dedupe.py must pass, and don't edit the test file.
